items.title: Small Caps Soar, Apple Slips, Treasury Yields Fall On Trump Day 2: What's Driving Markets Tuesday?
items.author: Piero Cingari
items.description: The Russell 2000 index led the market, rallying 1.7%, while other large-cap indices trailed behind. Apple Inc. plunged 4.3% after a downgrade, and Treasury yields fell as investors were convinced by Trump's pledges on inflation control and lower federal spending.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Apple Inc. plunged 4.3%, marking its worst trading day since early August 2024, following a downgrade by Jefferies citing challenges in iPhone sales in China.
The Russell 2000 index, which tracks small-cap stocks, rallied 1.7%, leading the market as investors bet on more domestic-friendly policies following Donald Trump's inauguration.
The Dow Jones index, composed of non-tech blue-chip companies, rose by 1.2%.

items.title: US Stocks Set To Open On A Positive Note As Trump's Second White House Term Kicks Off: Tesla, Apple, Netflix Among Stocks In Focus
items.author: Rounak Jain
items.description: US stocks are expected to open on a positive note as Donald Trump begins his second term as president. Key stocks in focus include Tesla, Apple, Netflix, and others. The market is optimistic about earnings growth prospects for US companies compared to their European counterparts.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The article mentions that Tesla was among the top gainers on Friday, surging 3% during the session.
Apple stock fell nearly 2% after a rare downgrade from Jefferies, citing slowing revenue growth and iPhone demand.
Netflix is mentioned as one of the stocks in focus, but no specific sentiment is provided.
The Invesco QQQ ETF rose 0.50% in premarket trading, indicating a positive sentiment.
The SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust gained 0.45% in premarket trading, indicating a positive sentiment.
